mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and lifts the S&P 500 over the subsequent month. The mechanism is always active given the stable recovery context.
failure_condition: RSAFS year-over-year growth exceeds 3% yet the S&P 500 posts negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances over any 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below the 5.0% threshold, it signals a tight labor market that may prompt wage inflation and preemptive monetary tightening, causing equity prices to decline over the next quarter.
failure_condition: After UNRATE drops below 5.0%, the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than half of the triggered months, contradicting the negative prediction.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation combined with a low federal funds rate indicates that real interest rates are becoming negative, which historically precedes Fed tightening and depresses equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: In months where both conditions hold, the S&P 500 20-day Sharpe ratio is not negative or the proportion of positive returns exceeds 55%, rejecting the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exhibit regime-dependent predictive sign. In the current recovery phase (HOUST below 1 million annualized), further increases reflect strengthening demand that supports corporate profits and drives positive long-term equity returns.
failure_condition: During regime where HOUST < 1M, a one-standard-deviation increase in HOUST is followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns in at least 50% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive momentum in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls signals broad economic strength that boosts corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY > 2% and PAYEMS MoM change > 100,000
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the S&P 500 20-day return is negative more than 40% of the time over any consecutive 12-month evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above a 2.5% threshold acts as a hawkish surprise that tightens financial conditions, triggering a short-term risk-off move in equities over the following week.
failure_condition: On days when FEDFUNDS first exceeds 2.5%, the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 60% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects a healthy manufacturing sector and rising capacity utilization, which historically translates into higher corporate earnings and positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: When INDPRO is above its 12-month moving average, the S&P 500 60-day return is negative in more than 35% of overlapping quarters over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In the mid-cycle labor market (unemployment rate between 4.5% and 5.5%), a monthly decline in unemployment of more than 0.1 percentage point signals improving job prospects without immediate wage pressure, supporting consumer spending and positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE declines more than 0.1pp while in the mid-cycle regime, the S&P 500 20-day return is positive in less than 50% of occurrences over any 2-year rolling window.
